summary.narrative: Francis Suarez delivered on several high-profile initiatives including positioning Miami as a tech and cryptocurrency hub, maintaining low taxes, and improving city infrastructure. However, affordable housing remained a persistent challenge throughout his tenure, and some sustainability goals fell short of targets. His presidential campaign suspended in August 2023 as promised when polling thresholds weren't met.

promises.text: Make Miami a major tech and innovation hub
promises.evidence: Successfully attracted major tech companies and startups to Miami, hosted major tech conferences, and personally recruited Silicon Valley firms via social media campaigns starting in 2020-2021.

promises.text: Establish Miami as a cryptocurrency-friendly city
promises.evidence: Launched MiamiCoin cryptocurrency initiative in 2021, accepted Bitcoin for city payments, and hosted the Bitcoin 2021 conference, making Miami a recognized crypto hub.

promises.text: Keep Miami taxes low with no city income tax
promises.evidence: Miami maintained no city income tax throughout Suarez's tenure, and he consistently promoted this as a draw for businesses and residents relocating from higher-tax states.

promises.text: Address affordable housing crisis in Miami
promises.evidence: Despite creating affordable housing programs and trust funds, Miami's housing affordability crisis worsened during his tenure with rents and home prices increasing dramatically, outpacing wage growth.

promises.text: Make Miami a climate-resilient city with sustainability initiatives
promises.evidence: Implemented some resilience projects including sea-level rise preparations and stormwater improvements, but comprehensive climate goals faced funding and implementation challenges.
Oversaw significant infrastructure investments including road improvements, the expansion of the Underline linear park project, and various neighborhood improvement initiatives.

promises.text: Suspend presidential campaign if failing to meet 1% polling threshold
promises.evidence: Suarez publicly committed to dropping out if he failed to reach polling thresholds. He suspended his campaign on August 29, 2023, becoming the first Republican to exit the 2024 race.
